<!-- title: Bitmap (TeaScript) -->
<!-- pageid: 1146 -->
<!-- revid: 10391 -->
<!-- timestamp: 2026-04-10T15:40:55Z -->
<!-- source: https://wohlsoft.ru/pgewiki/Bitmap_(TeaScript) -->

Bitmap is a class in TeaScript.vbs that allows you to read/write values of bitmaps.

=Creating a Bitmap=
To create a bitmap you can use the <code>BmpCreate</code>

<syntaxhighlight lang="vb">
call bmpcreate(index, picid, useScreenCoords, isVisible, sx, sy, sw, sh, destx, desty, stretchx, stretchy, centerx, centery, angle, color)
</syntaxhighlight>

For more information visit the [[Functions_(TeaScript)#BmpCreate|BmpCreate ]] documentation.
=Using the Bitmap Class=
To read the value of <code>Bitmap</code> use:
<syntaxhighlight lang="vb">
Bitmap(index).name
</syntaxhighlight>

To change the value of <code>Bitmap</code> use:
<syntaxhighlight lang="vb">
Bitmap(index).name = value
</syntaxhighlight>

Where index is an unique identifier defined when creating the bitmap object.

=Bitmap Properties=
=====Physical Properties=====
{{Tea_table|1=  
  {{Tea_row|double|destx|-|The x position of the bitmap.}}
  {{Tea_row|double|desty|-|The y position of the bitmap.}}
  {{Tea_row|double|scalex|-|The horizontal stretch ration. (e.g. 1: default size, 2:double size, 0.5: half size}}
  {{Tea_row|double|scaley|-|The horizontal stretch ration. (e.g. 1: default size, 2:double size, 0.5: half size}}
  {{Tea_row|double|rotatang|-|Rotates the bitmap in counter-clockwise in radians.}}
}}

=====Behavioral Properties=====
{{Tea_table|1=  
  {{Tea_row|double|attscreen|-|Determines if the bitmap uses scene or screen coordinates (0:scene, 1: screen).}}
  {{Tea_row|double|scrx|-|Determines the top left x position in the source image.}}
  {{Tea_row|double|scry|-|Determines the top left y position in the source image.}}
  {{Tea_row|double|scrwidth|-|Determines the width loaded in the source image.}}
  {{Tea_row|double|scrheight|-|Determines the height loaded in the source image.}}
  {{Tea_row|double|rotatx|-|The x position of the anchor used for scaling and rotating.}}
  {{Tea_row|double|rotaty|-|The y position of the anchor used for scaling and rotating.}}
}}

=====Graphical Properties=====
{{Tea_table|1=  
  {{Tea_row|double|hide|-|Determines if the bitmap is rendered (0: show, 1:hide)}}
  {{Tea_row|double|scrid|-|Determines the ID of the NPC used as the texture source.}}
  {{Tea_row|double|zpos|-|The priority of the bitmap. Foreground is 0 and background is 1.}}
  {{Tea_row|double|blendmode|-|Determines the blendmode of the bitmap. [Values listed below]}}
  {{Tea_row|double|forecolor|-|An overlay hue added to the bitmap. Setting this value to 0 makes the player invisible, setting it to -1 returns it to default. Use the <code>rgba(red, green, blue, alpha)</code> function to choose a specific hue.}}
  {{Tea_row|double|forecolor_r|-|The red value of the hue described in 'forecolor'. Should be a value between 0 to 255. Use this value to read and get only the red component.}}
  {{Tea_row|double|forecolor_g|-|The green value of the hue described in 'forecolor'. Should be a value between 0 to 255. Use this value to read and get only the green component.}}
  {{Tea_row|double|forecolor_b|-|The blue value of the hue described in 'forecolor'. Should be a value between 0 to 255. Use this value to read and get only the blue component.}}
  {{Tea_row|double|forecolor_a|-|The alpha value of the hue described in 'forecolor'. Should be a value between 0 to 255. Use this value to read and get only the alpha component.}}
}}
=Constants=
Here are a list of constants used by the Bitmap class

{| class="wikitable"
|+Blendmode Values
!ID
!Description
|-
|1
|Black
|-
|3
|Reddish
|-
|4
|Negative Colors
|-
|9
|Blue Cyan
|-
|10
|Orange/Red
|-
|1289
|Brighter Color
|-
|66050
|Additive
|-
|66053
|Additive + Alpha
|-
|197122
|Subtractive
|-
|197125
|Subtractive + Alpha
|}

=Oddities and Quirks=
Below is a list of certain quirks bitmaps have that does not conform to standard behavior or simply other miscellaneous behavior.


[[Category:TeaScript.vbs]]
